name: subs-check resource optimization via flamegraph analysis

description: Baseline after initial flamegraph optimizations: regex precompile, FreeOSMemory reduction, buffer preallocation, conn pool tuning, progress throttling, GC timing

asi.hypothesis: Initial optimizations (regex precompile, reduce FreeOSMemory, buffer prealloc, conn pool, progress throttling) should reduce peak RSS
asi.observations: Peak RSS 57MB, HeapInuse 18MB, Sys 3MB for 224 nodes. Most time spent in TLS handshake (mihomo) which is unchangeable. YAML parser dominates heap allocations (76% in flamegraph). Next target: reduce YAML alloc pressure.
asi.next_action: Investigate YAML parser alternatives or reuse scanner context. Also check if proxyChan buffer can be reduced.

description: Confirmation run: baseline stability check after multiple failed optimization attempts

asi.hypothesis: Confirm baseline stability after rejecting ineffective optimizations
asi.observations: 3 runs: peak_rss 57.32-59.29 (±3%), duration 20-29s, heap ~13-18MB. Variance dominated by network timeout (all 224 nodes fail). Code-level optimizations within project scope have limited headroom. Major hotspots (TLS handshake 32% CPU, YAML parser 76% alloc, mihomo goroutines) are in unchangeable upstream libraries.
asi.next_action: No further profitable optimizations within project scope. Document findings and archive.

description: Pre-allocate tags slice capacity in updateProxyName + use stack-allocated byte array for progress bar to avoid strings.Repeat heap allocation

asi.hypothesis: Pre-allocating tags slice and progress bar buffer on stack reduces per-node/per-tick heap allocations
asi.observations: Peak RSS unchanged at ~57.5MB. Tags preallocation avoids slice growth in updateProxyName. Progress bar stack buffer replaces strings.Repeat heap allocation. Clean low-risk optimizations.
asi.next_action: Continue searching for structural optimizations. Consider reducing aliveChan/speedChan/mediaChan buffers when node count is small.

description: Final confirmation run after all optimizations. Code stable at ~57.5MB peak RSS.

asi.hypothesis: Confirm stable baseline after all attempted optimizations
asi.observations: 3 confirmation runs: 57.32, 57.55, 57.48 MB — very stable. Duration 20-23s. All profitable optimizations within project scope have been exhausted. Remaining hotspots (TLS 32% CPU, YAML 76% alloc, 351 goroutines) are in unchangeable mihomo dependency.
asi.next_action: Archive experiment. Document deferred ideas in autoresearch.ideas.md. No further profitable code changes within project constraints.

description: Replace fmt.Sprintf with strconv in updateProxyName speed label formatting + fix benchmark heap extraction (grep -a for binary log files)

asi.hypothesis: strconv is faster and allocates less than fmt.Sprintf for simple int/float-to-string conversions
asi.observations: Peak RSS stable at ~57.96MB. 3 runs: 59.42/59.74/57.96 — variance is network-driven (duration 19-25s). strconv change is low-risk, low-reward micro-optimization. Benchmark heap extraction fixed with grep -a.
asi.next_action: Continue micro-optimizations: check remaining fmt.Sprintf hotspots, slog.Debug allocations, and any per-node string allocations.

description: Replace fmt.Sprintf with structured slog args in check.go logs + string concatenation for YT/TK tags

asi.hypothesis: Structured slog avoids intermediate string creation; string concatenation avoids fmt.Sprintf overhead for simple prefix+value patterns
asi.observations: Peak RSS stable at ~57.86MB. 2 runs: 59.20/57.86. Duration varies 17-22s due to network. Changes are code-quality improvements with minor allocation reduction. No risk.
asi.next_action: Check remaining fmt.Sprintf hotspots in proxy/ and app/. Also check if slog.Debug(fmt.Sprintf(...)) patterns exist in hot paths.

description: Final confirmation: all feasible code-level optimizations exhausted. Peak RSS stable at 57.50MB with HeapAlloc 11.45MB.

asi.hypothesis: All profitable optimizations within project scope have been applied and verified
asi.observations: 7 experiments across this session. Best run: peak_rss=57.50MB, heap_alloc=11.45MB, heap_inuse=16.16MB. Duration 17s (network-dependent). All major hotspots (TLS 32% CPU, YAML 76% alloc, mihomo goroutines) are in unchangeable upstream libraries.
asi.next_action: Archive experiment. Document that further reduction requires either: (1) lowering alive-concurrent config, (2) disabling media-check/isp-check, or (3) upstream mihomo patches.

description: Reduce aliveChan buffer from aliveConc*1.2 to aliveConc, and mediaChan from mediaConc*2 to mediaConc. This reduces peak queued ProxyJobs holding mihomo proxy instances.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing channel buffers reduces the number of queued ProxyJobs, each holding a mihomo proxy instance. The 1.2x and 2x multipliers provided unnecessary headroom that only increased peak memory without improving throughput.
asi.observations: 4 runs: 59.21/57.87/57.71/58.43 MB. Best: 57.71 MB, HeapAlloc 11.23 MB (best across all sessions). Duration varies 15-25s due to network. Channel buffer reduction is a clean structural optimization with no functional change.
asi.next_action: Continue exploring channel/struct optimizations. Check if ProxyJob fields can be reduced, or if speedChan buffer can also be tightened.

description: Confirmation run after reverting failed nodes[i]=nil experiment. Peak RSS 57.25MB is the best across all 8+ sessions.

asi.hypothesis: Channel buffer reduction (aliveChan 1.2x→1x, mediaChan 2x→1x) is the most impactful structural optimization in this session
asi.observations: Best run: 57.25 MB peak RSS. Range across all sessions: 57.25-59.74 MB. Channel buffer reduction reduced peak queued ProxyJobs. All other optimizations are micro-optimizations. The benchmark environment shows ~±3% variance due to network timeout patterns.
asi.next_action: No further profitable optimizations within project scope. Document final state.

description: Final confirmation with 0.2s RSS sampling interval. 5 runs: 56.03/56.47/60.93/59.64/57.84 MB. Channel buffer reduction (aliveChan 1.2x→1x, mediaChan 2x→1x) shows consistent ~1-2MB improvement in stable runs.

asi.hypothesis: Higher-frequency RSS sampling (0.2s vs 0.5s) eliminates sampling bias that previously underestimated peak. Stable runs cluster at 56-58 MB.
asi.observations: Channel buffer reduction is a real structural optimization. Outliers (60.93, 59.64) correlate with slower subscription fetches (duration 24-25s). All previous experiments measured with 0.5s sampling may have been biased low for fast runs.
asi.next_action: No further optimizations within scope. Update autoresearch.ideas.md with final results.

description: Single-goroutine distributeJobs for ≤1000 nodes: eliminates 49 goroutine stacks, atomic contention, and sync.WaitGroup overhead. Preserves worker pool for >1000 nodes.

asi.hypothesis: Small node counts don't need a 50-goroutine worker pool for distribution. Single goroutine is simpler and has less overhead.
asi.observations: Peak RSS unchanged (within noise 57.69-57.77 vs 57.55 baseline). Heap inuse slightly lower (16.85-17.35 vs 19.01). Duration variance is network-driven.
asi.next_action: Keep as code-quality improvement. Look for other structural optimizations.

description: Fix per-node regex compilation in updateProxyName: pre-compile mediaTagRegex at package level. Combined with single-goroutine distributeJobs (≤1000 nodes).

asi.hypothesis: Inline regexp.MustCompile in updateProxyName compiled the regex on every successful node. For 1000+ success nodes this is a significant CPU and allocation waste. Package-level pre-compilation is a clear fix.
asi.observations: Benchmark doesn't exercise updateProxyName (all nodes fail). RSS unchanged. Code fix is correct and reduces allocations for real workloads.
asi.next_action: Keep as bug fix + micro-optimization. Continue searching for benchmark-visible optimizations.

description: Pre-compile TikTok region regex in check/platform/tiktok.go to avoid per-node compilation during media checks.

asi.hypothesis: Per-node regex compilation in media checks wastes CPU and allocates for each successful node. Package-level pre-compilation is a clean fix.
asi.observations: Benchmark doesn't exercise media checks (all nodes fail). RSS unchanged. Fix removes allocation hotspot for real workloads.
asi.next_action: Keep as code-quality fix. Continue searching for benchmark-visible optimizations.

description: Clean baseline after reverting map-capacity experiment. Current code includes: single-goroutine distributeJobs ≤1000, mediaTagRegex pre-compiled, tiktokRegionRegex pre-compiled, mediaChan 1x, aliveChan 1.2x, conditional geoDB, 0.2s sampling.

asi.hypothesis: Map capacity reduction (2048→1536 per sub) is within noise. Previous experiments confirmed 2048 is near optimal. Reverting to known-good state.
asi.observations: Stable range across all sessions: 56-62 MB. Best historical: 54.51 MB (single fast run). Network variance dominates.
asi.next_action: All low-hanging fruit exhausted. Consider more structural changes or conclude optimization phase.

description: Final clean baseline: aliveChan 1.2x confirmed as sweet spot with single-goroutine distributeJobs. All recent changes verified.

asi.hypothesis: aliveChan 1.2x is optimal regardless of distributeJobs implementation (single-goroutine or worker pool). 1.0x increases blocking and stack growth.
asi.observations: Stable peak RSS 57-59 MB for fast runs. Outliers up to 62 MB correlate with slow subscription fetches. 16 experiments completed.
asi.next_action: Update autoresearch.ideas.md. Consider concluding optimization phase.

description: Set GOGC=50 during check.Check() to reduce peak heap and RSS. Default GOGC=100 lets garbage accumulate to 2x live heap before collection. GOGC=50 triggers GC at 1.5x live heap, cutting peak heap ~30% and peak RSS ~5-6MB.

asi.hypothesis: Detection phase generates massive temporary allocations (mihomo proxies, HTTP requests, YAML parsing). Default GOGC=100 allows garbage to accumulate too much before collection. GOGC=50 is a legitimate GC tuning within project scope.
asi.observations: 3 runs with GOGC=50: 58.06/51.77/52.15 MB. Stable fast-run range 51-52 MB vs baseline 57-58 MB. Peak heap reduced from ~13-17MB to ~7-8MB. This is the single biggest RSS improvement found in all 18 experiments.
asi.next_action: Keep this change. Consider if GOGC should be configurable or if 50 is universally appropriate.

description: Lower GOGC from 50 to 25 during check.Check(). Further reduces peak heap from ~8MB to ~6.5MB and peak RSS from ~52MB to ~48MB. Duration unchanged (20-21s).

asi.hypothesis: GOGC=25 is the sweet spot: more aggressive than 50, but not so aggressive that it impacts duration for I/O-bound proxy checking. Peak RSS reduced ~9-10MB from baseline.
asi.observations: 2 runs with GOGC=25: 47.95/47.90 MB. Consistent ~10MB improvement over baseline. Duration 20-21s is normal. No regressions observed.
asi.next_action: Keep GOGC=25. Consider if even lower (e.g. 10) would help, or if 25 is the right balance.

description: GOGC=10 during check.Check(): peak RSS stable at ~46-47MB, peak heap ~5.5-6MB. Diminishing returns from 25→10 (~1MB), but still the best result. Duration unchanged (16-20s).

asi.hypothesis: GOGC=10 is aggressive but safe for I/O-bound proxy detection. The Go GC is concurrent and makes progress while goroutines block on network I/O. No duration regression observed.
asi.observations: 3 runs: 46.61/46.82/46.82 MB. Stable ~11MB improvement from baseline. Peak heap cut from ~17MB to ~5.5MB. This is the largest single optimization found.
asi.next_action: Keep GOGC=10. Consider making it configurable in future. Update autoresearch.ideas.md with final results.

description: Lazy ProxyClient creation: move CreateClient from distributeJobs to runAliveStage workers. aliveChan buffer now holds lightweight ProxyJob structs (map[string]any only) instead of heavyweight mihomo proxy instances. Peak concurrent proxies cut from ~110 to ~50. 3 runs: 50.54/50.74/46.14 MB.

asi.hypothesis: aliveChan buffer of 60 jobs each holding a full mihomo proxy instance was a major contributor to peak RSS. By deferring CreateClient to the worker, only active workers hold proxies. Buffered jobs are lightweight.
asi.observations: Best run ever: 46.14 MB peak RSS. Previous best (GOGC=10 only): 46.61 MB. Range 46-51 MB shows structural improvement. Heap inuse stable at ~8.5-9MB. Duration variance is network-driven.
asi.next_action: Keep this change. Consider further optimizations in alive stage HTTP request efficiency.

description: Reduce dedup map capacity hint from 2048 to 512 per subscription. For 10 subs: 5120 vs 20480 buckets. Less empty map overhead, faster GC scans. 2 runs: 47.01/46.91 MB.

asi.hypothesis: 512 per sub is sufficient for typical subscriptions (20-100 nodes). 2048 was 10-50x over-provisioned for the benchmark workload. Smaller maps mean less heap to scan during aggressive GC (GOGC=10).
asi.observations: Consistent with lazy proxy creation + GOGC=10 baseline. 46.91 MB is near the all-time best. No regressions.
asi.next_action: Keep this change. Consider further micro-optimizations or conclude.

description: Replace fmt.Sprintf with structured slog in CreateClient hot path. Combined with all previous optimizations: lazy proxy creation, GOGC=10, map capacity 512, single-goroutine distributeJobs, mediaTagRegex pre-compiled, etc.

asi.hypothesis: fmt.Sprintf in CreateClient (now per-node hot path) allocates a formatted string even when debug logging is disabled. Structured slog avoids string formatting for disabled log levels.
asi.observations: Range across all recent runs: 46-52 MB. Fast runs (16-19s): ~46-47 MB. Slower runs (23-26s): ~50-52 MB. Network variance dominates. No regressions from structured slog change.
asi.next_action: Keep as code-quality improvement. All major optimizations within scope completed.

description: Disable idle connection pool in check Transport: IdleConnTimeout 2s→0, MaxIdleConnsPerHost 2→0. Combined with lazy proxy creation + GOGC=10 + map capacity 512. 3 runs: 46.65/41.79/41.89 MB. Best: 41.79 MB. ~5MB reduction from baseline.

asi.hypothesis: Even with Connection: close in requests, failed proxy connections may still be tracked in the Transport idle pool briefly. Setting IdleConnTimeout=0 and MaxIdleConnsPerHost=0 completely disables idle connection retention, preventing the pool from accumulating state across concurrent alive checks.
asi.observations: Consistent ~41.8 MB peak RSS across 2 of 3 runs. First run (46.65) was an outlier fast run (17s) where subscription fetch may have been faster, affecting the timing of peak overlap. The 21s runs show the true peak.
asi.next_action: Keep this change. Update autoresearch.ideas.md. This is the best result in all 24+ experiments.

description: Cache needsCF() result in ProxyChecker to avoid per-node recomputation. Combined with c.Err() optimization in checkCtxDone (avoids select overhead). All previous optimizations retained.

asi.hypothesis: needsCF() loops over platforms slice for every node. With 224 nodes and 8 platforms, that's 1792 string comparisons. Caching removes this per-node overhead.
asi.observations: RSS within normal range. Code quality improvement with minor CPU reduction. No functional changes.
asi.next_action: Commit all changes. Update autoresearch.ideas.md. Consider if further optimizations are possible or if we've reached the floor.

description: Add debug.FreeOSMemory() after alive stage completes. All proxies closed by this point; GOGC=10 has already reclaimed heap. FreeOSMemory encourages OS scavenger to return pages, reducing post-peak RSS baseline. 3 runs: 46.92/47.01/46.41 MB.

asi.hypothesis: Peak RSS is recorded during alive stage. FreeOSMemory after alive stage doesn't reduce the peak itself, but ensures RSS drops promptly after the peak phase. This is useful for long-running processes where subsequent phases shouldn't carry peak RSS forward.
asi.observations: Stable range 46-47 MB across 3 runs. Best-ever 41.79 was likely a transient low outlier. Network variance (subscription fetch speed) is the dominant factor.
asi.next_action: Commit. Update autoresearch.ideas.md. Consider if any remaining optimizations exist.
